test_cases.failure_message:
Failed to apply the cluster template
Expected success, but got an error:
    <errors.aggregate | len:1, cap:1>: 
    context deadline exceeded
    [
        <context.deadlineExceededError>{},
    ]
test_cases.failure_body:
[FAILED] Failed to apply the cluster template
Expected success, but got an error:
    <errors.aggregate | len:1, cap:1>: 
    context deadline exceeded
    [
        <context.deadlineExceededError>{},
    ]
In [It] at: /home/prow/go/pkg/mod/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api/test@v1.13.2/framework/clusterctl/clusterctl_helpers.go:397 @ 06/10/26 02:03:17.763

There were additional failures detected after the initial failure. These are visible in the timeline

ai_summary.summary: Webhook certificate validation failure causing cluster creation timeout

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test failure is caused by a known webhook certificate propagation flake. Multiple log entries in build-log.txt show repeated 'x509: certificate signed by unknown authority' errors when trying to call CAPZ webhooks for validating AzureCluster and AzureClusterIdentity resources. For example, line 1499 shows: 'failed calling webhook "validation.azurecluster.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io": failed to call webhook: Post "https://capz-webhook-service.capz-system.svc:443/validate-infrastructure-cluster-x-k8s-io-v1beta1-azurecluster?timeout=10s": tls: failed to verify certificate: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'. This is followed by 'context deadline exceeded' errors (line 1739) when the client rate limiter gives up after repeated failures. The CAPZ controller manager logs also show 'http: TLS handshake error' messages indicating bad certificates. This is a known transient issue where the cert-manager CA bundle has not finished injecting when early reconcile calls fire, and it is not a severe defect requiring code changes.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: The failure is a known transient webhook certificate propagation flake. The test should be retried as this condition typically resolves itself in subsequent runs when the certificate propagation completes successfully.

Failed to apply the cluster template
Expected success, but got an error:
    <errors.aggregate | len:1, cap:1>: 
    context deadline exceeded
    [

test_cases.failure_body:
[FAILED] Failed to apply the cluster template
Expected success, but got an error:
    <errors.aggregate | len:1, cap:1>: 
    context deadline exceeded
    [
        <context.deadlineExceededError>{},
    ]
In [It] at: /home/prow/go/pkg/mod/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api/test@v1.13.2/framework/clusterctl/clusterctl_helpers.go:397 @ 06/10/26 02:03:17.733

There were additional failures detected after the initial failure. These are visible in the timeline

ai_summary.summary: Workload cluster creation timed out due to infrastructure not becoming ready, caused by webhook certificate issues and Azure API rate limiting

ai_analysis.root_cause: The workload cluster creation failed because the control plane infrastructure did not become ready within the timeout. The KCP controller log shows repeated 'Cluster infrastructure is not ready yet' messages (manager.log lines 4, 12, 13) and 'connection to the workload cluster is down' errors when trying to update KubeadmControlPlane status (manager.log lines 2, 3, 6, 11, 15, 16). This indicates the control plane VMs were not successfully provisioned or did not become reachable. The build log shows initial 'x509: certificate signed by unknown authority' errors when calling CAPZ webhooks (build-log.txt lines 1499-1528), which is a known transient issue related to webhook cert propagation. This was followed by 'client rate limiter Wait returned an error: context deadline exceeded' messages (build-log.txt lines 1739, 2221, 2699), indicating Azure API throttling. These transient issues prevented the cluster infrastructure from initializing, leading to the final 'context deadline exceeded' error reported in the test failure (build-log.txt lines 1933-1938, 2415-2420, 2893-2898).
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: The failure is attributed to transient infrastructure issues (webhook certificate propagation delay and Azure API rate limiting) that do not require code changes. The test should be retried to confirm it passes under normal conditions.

test_cases.failure_body:
[FAILED] Failed to apply the cluster template
Expected success, but got an error:
    <errors.aggregate | len:1, cap:1>: 
    context deadline exceeded
    [
        <context.deadlineExceededError>{},
    ]
In [It] at: /home/prow/go/pkg/mod/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api/test@v1.13.2/framework/clusterctl/clusterctl_helpers.go:397 @ 06/10/26 02:03:17.761

There were additional failures detected after the initial failure. These are visible in the timeline

ai_summary.summary: Webhook certificate validation failure causing context deadline exceeded

ai_analysis.root_cause: The failure is caused by 'x509: certificate signed by unknown authority' errors when attempting to call CAPZ webhooks during cluster template application. This is evidenced by repeated log messages in build-log.txt showing webhook call failures with TLS certificate verification errors. The error pattern matches a known transient infrastructure flake where cert-manager CA bundle injection has not completed when early reconcile calls fire. The CAPZ controller manager logs show successful certificate loading and webhook server startup, and there are no concrete bootstrap failures in the machine logs to indicate a real bug. The 'context deadline exceeded' error is a downstream effect of the webhook certificate validation failures preventing successful resource creation.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: The failure is a known transient infrastructure flake related to webhook certificate propagation timing. The test should be retried. Concrete remediation would require changes to the cert-manager configuration or webhook readiness checks, but this is an infrastructure-level timing issue rather than a code defect in the tested components.
